note: Canon framing (Erik 2026-07-07): before/through the Great Transition, power consolidation and unconstrained expression MANIFESTED fiction into the world. Things that were only stories for a long time became real locals — a Minecraft-logic pocket, the ENT race, the whole bestiary of fantasy. They concentrate in NATIVE DOMAINS (a place where that fiction's logic runs pure) but nothing prevents anything from being ANYWHERE. This gives the world truly novel locals and creatures on top of the axis-dispositions. Ties to the coordinate model: a manifest-domain is a place whose pole-disposition + a seed-fiction produced a self-consistent pocket-reality. Authored by Aevi (PO).
principle: The Transition was not only catastrophe — it was expression without a governor. Where consolidated power met an imagined thing hard enough, the imagined thing MANIFESTED: became a real local with its own rules, creatures, and logic. Some manifestations are whole domains running on a fiction's internal physics; some are single creatures or peoples loose in the world. The substrate (nanite/lattice) is the medium — it read intense collective imagination as structured will and enacted it. This is why the world can hold anything: the only limit was what could be imagined vividly and consolidated enough to run.

domainLogic.native_domain: A place where one manifested fiction runs PURE — its own internal physics, unbroken. The clearest, strangest, most dangerous expression of that fiction. (A Minecraft-pocket where matter is blocky and craftable; the deep ENT-forest where the tree-folk logic is total.)
domainLogic.diffusion: Nothing confines a manifestation to its native domain. Creatures wander; pockets bleed; a person of a manifested race can be born or travel anywhere. Encountering the ENT at Thornwake Glade (already seeded) is diffusion — a native-Ent presence far from the deep Ent-forest. The world is dense with things out of their home context.
domainLogic.consistency: Inside a native domain, the fiction's rules are LAW (in a Minecraft-pocket, you mine and craft by that logic and the world obeys). Outside, a diffused manifestation carries as much of its logic as it can sustain — an Ent is still an Ent anywhere, but the deep-forest's total green-logic thins with distance from the native domain. Ties to substrate density (will_expression_modality): manifestations run strongest where substrate is dense.
domainLogic.coordinate_tie: A manifest-domain sits at a coordinate like anywhere else, but with a SEED-FICTION overlaid — the pole-disposition shapes which fictions could manifest there (a chaos+creation place births generative build-worlds; a death+order place births something gothic and lawful). The Rune Dice read the disposition; a domain's seed-fiction is a further layer the GM reveals.

sampleManifestations.name: The Blocklands
sampleManifestations.nativeLogic: Matter here is discrete and craftable — the world resolves into workable units; you gather, combine, and build by a grammar that always works, and unbuild the same way. Day is safe, dark brings things. A Minecraft-logic pocket, pure at its heart.
sampleManifestations.diffusion: Crafters who learned the grammar carry a shadow of it out — they build faster, cleaner, and can improvise structures the way no one else can. Rare 'block-touched' tools surface far from the Blocklands.
sampleManifestations.danger: Native domain is safe by day, lethal by night, by its own unbreakable rules; learn the rules and live, break them and don't.

sampleManifestations.name: The Deepwood (native ENT domain)
sampleManifestations.nativeLogic: The pure Ent-forest — trees that think in centuries, walk when they must, and hold grudges older than nations. Time runs slow and green; haste is the one true sin. Thornwake Glade is a far, thinned foothill of this.
sampleManifestations.diffusion: Ents wander (Thornwake's Old Stag-logic, Burr's grove-kinship); a person can be Ent-touched or Ent-born far from the Deepwood. ENT as a playable origin (already noted) is a diffused native.
sampleManifestations.danger: The Deepwood judges intent over centuries; a wrong done here is remembered until repaid, and the forest is patient.

sampleManifestations.name: The Bestiary (loose)
sampleManifestations.nativeLogic: The whole fantasy bestiary manifested — drakes, revenants, fae-things, golems, the lot — each concentrated in domains whose disposition birthed them (revenants where death+order co-fire; fae where chaos+life do) but loose everywhere. A drake over the Riven Marches, a fae-thing in the Churn, a golem left running in the Gearlands.
sampleManifestations.diffusion: Creatures are the most diffuse manifestation — encounter tables anywhere can include a manifested beast far from its native domain, flagged by how 'wrong for here' it reads.
sampleManifestations.danger: Varies; a diffused creature out of its domain is often MORE dangerous — unbound by its native rules and hungry in a place that has no defenses tuned for it.

buildImplication: Manifest-domains are authored like Reach locations but carry a `seedFiction` + `nativeLogic` field the GM enforces inside them. Creatures get a native-domain tag + a diffusion weight so encounter tables can place them out-of-context. Origins can include a manifested race (ENT already flagged; Blocklands-crafter, etc.). The GM treats a native domain's internal rules as LAW while inside it. This is a content layer + a light engine flag (seedFiction/nativeLogic on locations, native/diffusion on creatures) — flagged to the running systems spec. Aevi keeps authoring domains and manifested creatures as content; the enforcement flag is the only engine piece.
